content: Climate health vulnerability assessment considers: (1) Exposure - frequency/intensity of climate hazards (heat days, flood risk, fire zones); (2) Sensitivity - population characteristics (age, comorbidities, outdoor work); (3) Adaptive capacity - healthcare access, early warning systems, social safety nets. Vulnerable populations include: elderly (>65 years have 2-3x heat mortality risk), children (developing thermoregulation, longer lifetime exposure), low-income communities (limited AC, substandard housing), outdoor workers, those with chronic diseases. Measurement tools: Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) attributable to climate factors, excess mortality during extreme events, disease incidence trends in expanding endemic zones. Climate attribution studies increasingly quantify specific health impacts: 37% of heat-related deaths already attributable to anthropogenic climate change. Equity concerns: lowest-emitting countries (Sub-Saharan Africa) face highest health burdens.
